questions.question: What is the default value of align-items?
questions.explanation: The default value of align-items is stretch, which stretches items to fill the cross axis.

questions.question: Which axis does align-items control?
questions.explanation: align-items controls alignment on the cross axis.

questions.question: Which value aligns items by their text baseline?
questions.explanation: baseline aligns flex items by the baseline of their text content.

questions.question: How do you center items both horizontally and vertically in Flexbox?
questions.explanation: Use justify-content: center (main axis) and align-items: center (cross axis).

questions.question: Which property can override align-items on a specific flex item?
questions.explanation: align-self overrides the align-items value for an individual flex item.

questions.question: With flex-direction: row, what does align-items: flex-start do?
options: Aligns items to the top of the container
questions.explanation: With row, the cross axis is vertical, so flex-start aligns items to the top.

questions.question: What does stretch do when items have a fixed height?
questions.explanation: If items have a fixed height, stretch has no effect as the item already has a definite size.

questions.question: With flex-direction: column, what does align-items: center do?
questions.explanation: With column, the cross axis is horizontal, so center aligns items horizontally.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a valid align-items value?
questions.explanation: space-between is a justify-content value, not an align-items value.

questions.question: What happens when align-items: stretch is applied to items without explicit height?
options: Items stay at their natural height
Items stretch to fill container height
questions.explanation: stretch makes items fill the cross axis size of the container.

questions.question: Which value would you use for a vertically centered sidebar item?
questions.explanation: center aligns the item in the middle of the cross axis.

questions.question: Can align-items be different for each line when using flex-wrap?
options: No, align-items applies to all lines uniformly
Yes, each line can have different alignment
questions.explanation: align-items applies uniformly to all flex lines. Use align-content for multi-line distribution.

questions.question: What does align-self: auto do?
questions.explanation: align-self: auto inherits the align-items value from the parent container.

questions.question: Which aligns items at the bottom of the container?
questions.explanation: flex-end aligns items at the end of the cross axis (bottom for row).

questions.question: What is the difference between align-items and align-content?
options: align-items works on single line; align-content works on multi-line
questions.explanation: align-items aligns items within a line; align-content distributes multiple lines.

questions.question: With flex-direction: row-reverse, where does align-items: flex-end place items?
questions.explanation: The cross axis is still vertical for row-reverse, so flex-end places items at the bottom.

questions.question: What is a common use case for baseline alignment?
options: Aligning text in cards with different font sizes
questions.explanation: baseline is useful when items have different font sizes and you want text aligned uniformly.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct syntax?
questions.explanation: flex-start is the correct value; left, top, and start are not valid align-items values.

questions.question: When would you use align-self?
options: When all items need different alignment
When one item needs different alignment from others
questions.explanation: align-self overrides alignment for a single item while others follow align-items.

questions.question: Which two properties together create perfect centering?
questions.explanation: justify-content: center (main axis) + align-items: center (cross axis) creates perfect centering.
